title: trillium pusillum var. ozarkanum
intro: Trillium pusillum var. ozarkanum, also known as Ozark trillium or Ozark toadshade, is a rare and endangered perennial plant belonging to the trillium genus. It is native to the Ozark Mountains in the United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions like arthritis and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically used, and can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
